assignment_description:
⍞ Materials: The Plutarch Primer: Publicola

PREP: You may want to read a retelling so you can be familiar with the story. This is not to be read to the students.
∞ Link: Retelling of Publicola from Our Young Folks' Plutarch
• Read about Publicola and the intro material, p.11-14
• Pre-read the lesson so you are comfortable reading aloud.

➜ INTRO
1\. Plutarch was born in Greece a few years after the death of Christ in the year 45 AD and died around 120 AD. He said that his purpose was to write lives, not histories. A life was a portrait of a man's inner self, whereas a history was an account of men's actions and fortunes without focusing on what the people were like who produced them. Plutarch's Lives is a book of patterns, models on which men may mold themselves, and be inspired to do so. Greatness is Plutarch's theme, not great fortune—he loves to depict a hero in adversity. (From Introduction to Plutarch by Edith Hamilton, p.xv-xvii.)
2\. We are reading about a Roman man named Publicola who lived around 550 BC. His actual name was Publius Valerius, but his nickname became Publicola, which means a man of the people or loved by the people. Listen for the actions that caused him to be given that nickname. In the book, he will be referred to as Valerius.
3\. When this account begins, Rome is ruled by King Tarquin. But when his son's outrageous behavior caused a public scandal, the king was removed from the throne.
4\. Let's look at the characters. (See right-hand column. You may want to write their names on a whiteboard.)
5\. We will read about how the Romans went from a monarchy (king) to a republic with consuls as the main officials of the government.

➜ LOCATE
∞ Map Link: Map of the Regions of Italy
At this time, there was no unified country called Italy. It was made up of many city-states or regions. Find the region of Rome and the Sabini. What do you think the people from those areas are called? (Romans and Sabines) These two areas had been at war with each other.

➜ READ & NARRATE
Lesson 1, p.17-18 "Such was Solon" - "consul with Brutus."

➜ DISCUSS
Why do you think Valerius thought he would become the consul with Brutus?

CHARACTERS
Valerius aka Publicola

Tarquin-last king of Rome

Brutus-joined with Valerius to head up revolt against the king, considered founder of Roman Republic

DEFINITIONS
republic- A form of government in which the people elect, or choose, their leaders

consul-the chief civil and military official of the Roman Republic. Two were elected yearly and they presided over meetings of the senate and assemblies of the whole people and put into effect the decrees they enacted. In war they took command of the army.
assignment_teachersNote:
★ TEACHER TIP
You may want to write the definitions of the vocabulary words in pencil next to the words or in the margin so you can say the definition right after you read the word in the text. You may also want to write a few of the words on a whiteboard with a definition or synonym.

assignment_description:
⍞ Materials: The Plutarch Primer: Publicola

PREP: Review vocabulary and pre-read the lesson.

➜ RECAP
What did we read last time?

➜ INTRO
What might be hard for people to change from a monarchy to a republic, from a king to a government where people elect their leaders? We read last time that Valerius thought that he might become one of the consuls. Let's find out what happens. (Set up the scene by reviewing characters and going over vocabulary words. See Teacher's Notes.)
You may want to write the names on a whiteboard.

➜ READ & NARRATE
Lesson 1 p.18-19 "Howbeit this" - "sincerity of his oath."

➜ DISCUSS
• How does Valerius show his loyalty to the republic and not the former king, even though he wasn't chosen as consul?
• What does that say about his character?

CHARACTERS
Tarquinius Collatinus-Lucretia's husband

DEFINITIONS
solicitations-to urge something, such as one's cause strongly (p.18)

∞ Article: From Cicero to Trump, they're all in Plutarch's Lives

∞ Article: Teaching Plutarch in the Age of Hollywood

assignment_description:
⍞ Materials: The Plutarch Primer: Publicola

PREP: Read the introduction, review vocabulary, and pre-read the lesson. You may want to write names and select vocab words on a whiteboard.

➜ RECAP
What did we read last time?

➜ INTRO
Read aloud the intro in the book. Why might people be persuaded to help their king or let him come back? (Set up scene by reviewing any characters and explaining a couple of vocabulary words.)

➜ READ & NARRATE
Lesson 2, p.21-22 "For there came" - "out after them."

➜ DISCUSS
Why do you think Valerius did not want the ambassadors to talk to the people of Rome?

CHARACTERS
Collatinus- In this section, he is only referred to by this name. He is Tarquinius Collatinus, the co-consul and husband of Lucretia.

DEFINITIONS
ambassador- representative or messenger (p.21)

subsidies-gift of money (p.22)

suffer- to allow (p.22)

tyranny/tyrants-government in which absolute power is found in a single ruler
(p.22)

⍞ Materials: The Plutarch Primer: Publicola

PREP: Review vocabulary and pre-read the lesson. You may want to write names and select vocab words on a whiteboard.

➜ RECAP
What did we read last time?

➜ INTRO
We will be introduced to two families that were influential in Rome—the Aquillians and Vitellians. (Write these names on a whiteboard.) The ambassadors of King Tarquin were trying to win them over. The Vitellians were related to Brutus and tried to win over Brutus' sons. We will read what will happen.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Lesson 2 p.22-23 "The questions, however" - "at the consultation."

• Why were Brutus' sons persuaded to kill the consuls?

CHARACTERS
Collatinus- Tarquinius Collatinus, the co-consul and husband of Lucretia.

Vindicius- a slave who overhears an important conversation

DEFINITIONS
entrails-internal parts (p.23)
assignment_teachersNote:
★ TEACHER TIP
Plutarch's writing can be confusing at times because of his overuse of pronouns. Sometimes it gets confusing to determine what antecedent the pronoun refers to. It might be helpful in those places to replace the pronouns with the actual nouns.

Titus & Tiberius-Brutus's sons

DEFINITIONS
lictors-the bodyguards of the consuls, made up of twelve men. Each lictor carried a bundle of sticks (rods) that also contained an axe (p.27)

⍞ Materials: The Plutarch Primer: Publicola

PREP: Read the introduction, review vocabulary, and pre-read the lesson. You may want to write names and select vocab words on a whiteboard.

➜ RECAP
What did we read last time?

➜ INTRO
Read the intro in the book aloud. (Set up scene by explaining a couple of vocabulary words. You may want to write the names on a whiteboard.)

➜ LOCATE
∞ Map Link: Map of Regions of Italy
As mentioned before, there were many different people groups that made up Italy at this time. We will read about the Tuscans. They lived in a part of the region that is labeled "Etrurians." Find that on the map.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Lesson 5 p.33-34 "Tarquinius then being" - "it did afterwards."

• What do you think that "divine announcement" could have been? (See note in book on p.34)

➜ VIEW
Look at this picture that depicts a Roman triumph. A Roman Triumph was a spectacular celebration parade held in the ancient city of Rome for a military commander who had won an important victory on the battlefield. Plutarch says that this victory over the Tuscans marked the first Roman Triumph.
∞ Image Link: Image of a Roman Triumph

DEFINITIONS
enmity- mutual hatred or ill will (p.33)

adversaries-enemies or opponents (p.33)

ILLUSTRATE- Draw a picture of the Roman Triumph using watercolors or pastels.

⍞ Materials: The Plutarch Primer: Publicola

PREP: Read the introduction, review vocabulary, and pre-read the lesson. You may want to write names and select vocab words on a whiteboard.

➜ RECAP
What did we read last time?

➜ INTRO
Now that Brutus has died, Valerius is the only consul until a new one is elected. The people thought that Valerius was acting too much like a king in various ways. Let's read to find out how Valerius responds to these criticisms.

➜ READ, NARRATE,
Lesson 6 p.36-37 "The people applauded" - "of Vica Pota."

➜ NARRATE
This is a good section to act out the narration

➜ DISCUSS
It says that the people "expressed their wonder and their respect for Valerius's magnanimity." Magnanimity means generosity of spirit. To be magnanimous is to be noble and to have a greatness of mind and heart. This is how good leaders should be characterized. Charlotte Mason would say that good citizens should be magnanimous people. How did Valerius show magnanimity in this reading?

⍞ Materials: The Plutarch Primer: Publicola

PREP: Review vocabulary and pre-read the lesson.

➜ RECAP
What did we read last time?

➜ INTRO
While Valerius was waiting for another consul to be elected, he used his authority to make some changes to the government and laws.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Lesson 6 p.37-38 "Now because" - "and porci, hogs."

• How did Publicola show that he was a leader who cared about the people and not his own power?

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to the course.

Questions will come from:
The Plutarch Primer: Publicola

Tarquin has not given up yet. He gets help from a prince in Italy named Lars Porsena. We will see what happens when they oppose the Romans. (Set up the lesson by reviewing the names and putting them on the whiteboard.)

➜ LOCATE
We have looked at the region of Etruria in other lessons. Now let's look closer and find the city of Clevsin. That is the Estruscan name for it. The Roman name is Clusium, and a powerful prince of Italy lived there who wanted to help Tarquin regain his power.
∞ Map Link: Map of Ancient Etruria

∞ PDF Link: Horatius at the Bridge (abridged)

Tarquin-last king of Rome

Lars Porsena-ruler of Clusium in Etruria

Titus Lucretius-new co-consul with Publicola

Horatius Cocles-helped defend the Romans

We are going to read about two different times when the Sabines attacked Rome. Part One is when Publicola is not consul. Part Two is when Publicola becomes consul for the fourth time. (Review the characters and explain vocabulary words. You may want to write the names on a whiteboard.)

➜ LOCATE
∞ Map Link: Map of the Regions of Italy
Find the region of Sabini. The people from there are called Sabines. They were briefly mentioned in the first lesson.

Postumius Tubertus-co-consul with Marcus

DEFINITIONS
eloquence- the art or power of speaking or writing in a forceful and convincing way (p.56)

★ Plutarch: Beginner Track — TERM 2 EXAM QUESTIONS
· The Plutarch Primer: Publicola: Pick one of the events to show how Publicola was a wise leader:
1. The situation with Porsena, Tarquin, and the Roman girls sent as hostages.
2. The situation with the Sabine man, Clausus, who was disliked by his fellow citizens for opposing war.
